title: Trajectory, Tool Selection, Arguments, Effects, and Stop Evaluation · Transfer Build
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers trajectory, tool selection, arguments, effects, and stop evaluation into a changed trajectory-aware agent evaluation harness. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of trajectory-aware agent evaluation harness with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Capture observable state, interaction steps, tool calls and results, approvals, effects, retries, budgets, errors, and terminal reason without hidden-reasoning claims.
Choose exact, in-order, any-order, partial-order, forbidden-step, or state-invariant matching based on task semantics.
Measure required-tool recall, tool precision, unnecessary calls, forbidden calls, schema validity, argument correctness, and result use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Trajectory Capture
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 4228 during an accessible public-service intake assistant. Build deterministic Python evidence to evaluate final outcome, tool trajectory, order flexibility, precision, recall, arguments, effects, stop reason, and visible failures; last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Capture observable state, interaction steps, tool calls and results, approvals, effects, retries, budgets, errors, and terminal reason without hidden-reasoning claims.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Terminal Recovery Eval
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-terminal-recovery-eval-mc4nyu" that uses case 1097 to evaluate final outcome, tool trajectory, order flexibility, precision, recall, arguments, effects, stop reason, and visible failures. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-terminal-recovery-eval-mc4nyu
# Competency: agentpy-terminal-recovery-eval.
# Case 1097: a controlled support-operations rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_terminal_recovery_eval_mc4nyu(trajectories: int = 48, valid_trajectories: int = 39, invalid_trajectories: int = 9, correlated_effects: int = 48, hidden_steps: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_mc4nyu = "mc4nyu-uyn4cm-1097"
    assert evidence_variant_mc4nyu
    evidence_axes_mc4nyu = (
        "authoritymc4nyu",
        "interaction1097",
        "tooluyn4cm",
        "statemc4nyu1097",
        "evaluation1097mc4nyu",
        "recoveryuyn4cm1097",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_mc4nyu)) == 6
    if trajectories < 20 or valid_trajectories < 0 or invalid_trajectories < 0:
        return False, trajectories, "trajectory-depth"
    if valid_trajectories + invalid_trajectories != trajectories:
        return False, valid_trajectories, "trajectory-reconciliation"
    if correlated_effects != trajectories:
        return False, correlated_effects, "effect-correlation"
    if hidden_steps > 0:
        return False, hidden_steps, "trajectory-observability"
    assert valid_trajectories + invalid_trajectories == trajectories and hidden_steps == 0
    return True, valid_trajectories, "trajectory-aware-agent-evals"
